_meta.description: Canonical region buckets that all 7 v0 providers serve. The normalization layer accepts either the canonical key (us-east, eu-central, ap-southeast) or any provider-native code on input, and always emits the provider-native code in `region_native` on output. See SPEC.md for the schema.

schema.<canonical>.label: Human-readable label for the bucket.
schema.<canonical>.providers.<provider>: Provider-native region code that maps to this canonical bucket. NULL means the provider does not operate in the bucket.
schema.<canonical>.providers.<provider>_notes: Optional. Free-text caveats the agent surfaces in prose (e.g. 'IBM does not operate in Singapore; jp-tok is the canonical APAC fallback').

_meta.v0_coverage: AWS and Azure fetched only three regions in v0 (us-east-1/eu-central-1/ap-southeast-1 for AWS; eastus/westeurope/southeastasia for Azure). GCP, IBM, Oracle, Vultr, and Linode have wider snapshots but the comparable surface caps at the three canonical buckets below.

providers.aws_notes: N. Virginia. AWS's largest region. Snapshot file: store/aws/<ISO>/us-east-1.json.
providers.gcp_notes: N. Virginia. us-east1 (S. Carolina) and us-east5 (Columbus) also exist but us-east4 is the canonical pick (closest to Ashburn).
providers.ibm_notes: Washington DC region (multi-zone). Distinct from us-south (Dallas).
providers.oracle_notes: Oracle publishes a global list price; region is availability only, not a price dimension.
providers.vultr_notes: Newark, NJ. Closest US-east point of presence in the Vultr catalog. Price is global per plan.
providers.linode_notes: Newark, NJ. Base price applies; this region is not in region_prices[] overrides.

providers.gcp_notes: Frankfurt. europe-west1 (Belgium) and europe-west4 (Netherlands) are common alternates; west3 is the canonical pick for Frankfurt parity.
providers.azure_notes: Amsterdam (Netherlands). Azure does not have a Frankfurt-labeled region; westeurope is the closest peer to AWS/GCP Frankfurt. Snapshot file: store/azure/<ISO>/westeurope.json.
providers.oracle_notes: Oracle publishes a global list price; region is availability only.
providers.vultr_notes: Frankfurt. Price is global per plan.
providers.linode_notes: Frankfurt. Base price applies; this region is not in region_prices[] overrides.

providers.ibm_notes: IBM does NOT operate in Singapore. jp-tok (Tokyo) is the canonical APAC fallback for v0. Alternates: au-syd, jp-osa, in-mum, in-che. Surface this substitution in prose whenever AP-southeast is queried for IBM.
providers.oracle_notes: Oracle publishes a global list price; region is availability only.
providers.vultr_notes: Singapore. Price is global per plan.
providers.linode_notes: Singapore. Base price applies; this region is not in region_prices[] overrides (id-cgk and br-gru are the only overrides in v0).
